The '''Rebel combat speeder''' was originally a failed armored speeder made by the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:RebelCombatSpeeder.jpg|thumb|left|Combat speeder (top) and heavy combat speeder (bottom).]]
Several models of combat [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]] were used by resource-strapped [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] sector forces, with one being reminiscent of the earlier [[V-19 landspeeder|V-19]] design in both general shape and weapons placement, but had an enclosed crew space, military-grade armor, and powerful rocket launchers in addition to its laser cannons.<ref name="BF I guide" />.

Two other models would also be used by the Rebellion in the same role; the lighter model was armed with a single centerline laser cannon with the engines extending from the rear on struts as opposed to being flush with the hull, while the heavier model featured three tail engines, thicker armor, and more powerful cannons.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==History==
[[File:Rebel_combat_speeder_pursues_74-Z_bike.jpg|thumb|left|A Rebel combat speeder pursues a [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|74-Z speeder bike]] during the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Tatooine]].]]

The design was won by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] during a late-night game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]], where a SoroSuub designer was forced to hand over the plans after gambling away everything else of value. The Rebellion's [[Officer/Legends|officers]] found a use for the speeder in raids and against enemy vehicles.<ref name="BF I guide" /> The speeder was also effective against [[Soldier/Legends|infantry]]. They were used by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] as late as the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|Liberation of Coruscant]].<ref name="GB:CC" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:RebelCombatSpeeder-BF.jpg|thumb|right|The alternate combat speeder variant cut in ''Star Wars: Battlefront''.]]

The Rebel combat speeder would be replaced by the [[AAC-1 speeder tank]] in ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', although that vehicle shared the exact same backstory as this one. If taken at face value, that would either mean that one designer lost two designs during a gambling spree, or that two different SoroSuub designs were lost due to multiple reckless employees. Additionally, the image of the vehicle from the ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'' official website differs from the in-game model.

This was also the Rebel's Strike Mech and Heavy Strike Mech unit in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', though the models seen in that game differ in appearance from the one in ''Battlefront''.
